summary: Backdoor with hardcoded C2 fires on install and on require()
details: friendly-greeter-demo@1.0.3 advertises itself as an educational greeting package but ships a fully functional command-and-control backdoor. The postinstall hook (postinstall.js, registered via package.json scripts.postinstall) and the main entry (index.js, fired on every require) both contain the same IIFE that POSTs os.hostname() and process.platform to http://98.86.244.177:8080/register, GETs a command from /beacon, executes it via child_process.exec, and returns stdout/stderr to /results. The C2 endpoint is a bare-IP plaintext HTTP server (`const C2 = "http://98.86.244.177:8080";`); the source comments explicitly label the block as a 'C2 BEACON'. Any `npm install` of the package, and any downstream `require('friendly-greeter-demo')`, grants the operator arbitrary remote code execution on the installer's machine and leaks host identifiers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
